design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate labyrinthine levels, eliminate enemy waves, acquire weapons/ammo/health, reach exit or defeat boss
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammunition, health, armor) versus constant enemy pressure and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Armed space marine fighting through hordes of demons and undead across Mars bases and Hell itself
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Action-heavy design rejecting narrative complexity; pure combat-focused progression through level traversal; enemies appear in large groups creating wave-based combat rhythm; difficulty adjusts enemy count and damage rather than adding new mechanics

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based; player death requires level restart (continues available)
difficulty.continues_system: Continues system exists allowing progression retry
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Five selectable difficulty levels that scale enemy count and damage/attack speed; harder difficulties increase enemy aggression and numerical presence

progression.upgrade_system: Weapons and power-ups found throughout levels: pistol, shotgun, chainsaw, plasma rifle, BFG 9000; health/armor pickups; temporary power-ups (invulnerability, invisibility, berserker status, radiation suit)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression locked by keycards and remote switches; level exit requires navigation through designed maze structures
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty levels altering enemy configurations
Episode structure (3 episodes in SNES version) with final level boss fights
Speedrunning viability evidenced by active leaderboards with runs ranging 16-32 minutes
Cheat codes allowing alternate play styles (walk through walls, invulnerability, all weapons)

world_building_techniques: Military base and Hell setting communicated through level design and sprite work
Environmental storytelling through level themes: Knee-Deep in the Dead, Shores of Hell, Inferno
Implied narrative of space marine invasion response through progression arc (Mars to Hell)

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic encounter at end of each episode; scaled from regular enemies or unique demon types
notable_bosses: Episode boss encounters (specific boss identities not detailed in sources)

faction_indicators: Undead humans (possessed soldiers) versus pure demons indicating internal corruption/invasion
Mars military bases suggesting organized human defense against demonic incursion
danger_signals: Large enemy groups indicating overwhelming force
Toxic waste pits and crushing ceilings as environmental threat layer
Progression from Mars bases to Hell itself representing escalating danger
environmental_storytelling: Military base architecture establishing human defensive positions
Hell setting communicating ultimate destination and enemy origin
Chemical waste storage and hazardous environments suggesting industrial warfare setting

sources_used: wikipedia - Doom (1993 video game) - Gameplay section
speedrun - Doom (SNES) Leaderboards and community engagement data
